questions.question_text: All Kentucky real estate advertising must include what identifying information?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage name as registered with KREC
distractors: The individual agent's personal license number
The agent's personal phone number and email
The KREC advertising approval number
questions.explanation: All advertising must include the brokerage name as registered with KREC. This applies to all forms of advertising including print, internet, and social media.

questions.question_text: A sales associate posts a property listing on social media with only their personal name. What rule does this violate?
distractors: The state no-call list regulation
Team advertising disclosure rules
Electronic transaction filing standards
questions.explanation: All advertising, including social media posts, must include the brokerage name as registered with KREC. Advertising without brokerage identification is a blind ad violation.

questions.question_text: What is a 'blind ad' in Kentucky real estate?
distractors: An ad targeting specific demographics
An ad placed without KREC approval
An ad using a team name only
questions.explanation: A blind ad is any advertising that does not include the brokerage name. Blind ads are prohibited in Kentucky — all advertising must identify the brokerage.

questions.question_text: Under KREC's broad definition, which of the following qualifies as 'advertising'?
questions.correct_answer: Any method making services or property known
distractors: Only paid newspaper or magazine print ads
Only MLS listings and brokerage websites
Only ads specifically targeting licensed agents
questions.explanation: KREC defines advertising broadly as any manner, method, or activity by which a licensee makes known a property for sale/lease or services requiring a license.

questions.question_text: A broker places an ad on a real estate website showing only their team name. Is this compliant?
questions.correct_answer: No, the brokerage name must also appear
distractors: Yes, team names satisfy the requirement
Only if the team is registered with KREC
Yes, for internet advertising only
questions.explanation: Team names alone are not sufficient. All advertising, including team advertising, must include the brokerage name as registered with KREC.

questions.question_text: Under the 2019 regulation overhaul, what defines a 'team' in Kentucky real estate?
questions.correct_answer: Licensees working together under one broker
distractors: Any two agents sharing a listing
A broker and their sales associates
Agents from different brokerages partnering
questions.explanation: A team is a group of licensees working together, affiliated with the same principal broker, led by a team leader, and using the same authorized alternate or assumed name.

questions.question_text: A team wants to advertise under 'The Smith Group.' What additional requirement applies?
questions.correct_answer: The brokerage name must be included
distractors: KREC must approve the team name first
The team leader's license number is needed
No additional requirements for team ads
questions.explanation: All team advertising must include the brokerage name. The team name alone is not sufficient — the brokerage must always be identifiable in the advertisement.

questions.question_text: Who leads a real estate team under Kentucky's team regulations?
distractors: The principal broker of the office
The most senior licensed member
Any member elected by the team
questions.explanation: Kentucky team regulations require a designated team leader. The team operates under the principal broker's supervision but has its own internal leadership structure.

questions.question_text: A team member from one brokerage wants to join a team at a different brokerage while keeping their current affiliation. Is this permitted?
questions.correct_answer: No, team members must share a broker
distractors: Yes, with both brokers' written consent
Yes, if properly registered with KREC
Only for part-time or limited team roles
questions.explanation: All team members must be affiliated with the same principal broker. A licensee cannot be on a team at a different brokerage than the one they are affiliated with.

questions.question_text: A team uses multiple different trade names across various advertising platforms. Does this comply with KREC rules?
questions.correct_answer: No, teams must use one authorized name
distractors: Yes, if all are registered with KREC
Yes, for different geographic areas
Only if the brokerage name appears too
questions.explanation: Kentucky teams must use the same authorized alternate or assumed name. Using multiple different trade names across platforms would violate team naming requirements.

questions.question_text: A licensee calls a number on Kentucky's do-not-call registry to solicit listings. What has occurred?
distractors: An acceptable business practice
A violation only if calling after 9 PM
No violation for real estate calls
questions.explanation: Kentucky maintains a do-not-call registry, and licensees must comply. Calling registered numbers for solicitation purposes violates the no-call list regulation.

questions.question_text: Are electronic signatures valid for Kentucky real estate transactions?
distractors: No, wet signatures are always required
Only for contracts under $50,000
Only with KREC pre-approval
questions.explanation: Electronic signatures are valid under Kentucky's Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA). This allows real estate documents to be signed electronically.

questions.question_text: A licensee sends unsolicited marketing emails to past clients who are on the no-call registry. Is this a violation?
questions.correct_answer: No, the no-call list covers phone calls
distractors: Yes, all unsolicited marketing is covered
Only if the emails contain property listings
Only if sent more than once per month
questions.explanation: The do-not-call registry specifically applies to phone calls, not emails. However, licensees must still comply with federal CAN-SPAM Act requirements for email marketing.

questions.question_text: A buyer wants to sign a purchase agreement using a digital signature platform. What act authorizes this?
distractors: Kentucky Digital Commerce Act
Federal E-Sign Act exclusively
KREC Electronic Practices Rule
questions.explanation: Kentucky's adoption of the Uniform Electronic Transactions Act (UETA) authorizes electronic signatures for real estate transactions, making digital signing platforms valid.

questions.question_text: A licensee's existing client is on the no-call registry. Can the licensee call them about a new listing that matches their criteria?
distractors: No, the registry applies to all calls
Only with written consent each time
Only during standard business hours
questions.explanation: Do-not-call registries typically exempt existing business relationships. A licensee with an established client relationship may contact that client about relevant listings.
